role: Head of Artificial Intelligence & Board Member
summary: Brought in to fix a broken LoRA training pipeline at an AI-powered greeting card startup; ended up rebuilding the company's entire generative AI strategy, leading the AI engineering team, and guiding the 30-person startup to its MVP launch.
highlights: Diagnosed and rebuilt a broken Flux LoRA training pipeline — data curation, captioning strategy, and training hyperparameters
Engineer art prompts for frontier image models including NanoBanana 2 and ChatGPT-Image-1.5, in addition to Flux and Stable Diffusion workflows
Author system prompts for HeartStamp's AI agent, integrating the latest text-based LLM models into the core product experience
Build automated prompt regression test suites and golden datasets using LangSmith (LangChain) to ensure output quality as models and workflows evolve
Benchmark models for latency/cost trade-offs and maintain full prompt versioning in Git across all image and text integrations
Architected AI agent workflows and RAG data systems connecting generative image output to the product experience
Guided a 30-person startup from broken AI infrastructure to MVP launch in under six months
Elevated to Board Member in recognition of strategic contributions beyond the AI engineering scope

summary: Open-source AI agent harness published as @scanton/phase2s on npm. Lets developers use their existing ChatGPT subscription as a programmable coding assistant, plugs into Claude Code as an MCP server for cross-model adversarial review, and includes a 'dark factory' for autonomous long-horizon spec execution with parallel git worktrees.
highlights: Built a full AI coding agent harness with 29 built-in skills spanning the complete development loop: specify, plan, implement, test, review, debug, ship, deploy

Cross-model adversarial review: Claude Code calls Phase2S as an MCP tool, which runs the plan through GPT via ChatGPT subscription and returns structured VERDICT / STRONGEST_CONCERN / OBJECTIONS / APPROVE_IF output

Dark factory (phase2s goal): reads a spec, breaks it into sub-tasks, implements each via a retry-until-green loop, runs acceptance criteria, and re-plans failed sub-tasks with failure context — autonomously, without human supervision
Parallel execution engine: 3+ independent sub-tasks run in isolated git worktrees simultaneously, cutting execution time by up to 60%, with sequential merge and conflict detection
Named agent personas (Apollo/Athena/Ares) with hard-wired tool registries enforced at the capability level — not just system prompts — so a read-only Q&A agent literally cannot write files
Works on your existing ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription via Codex CLI browser auth — no API key, no per-token billing; also supports Anthropic, OpenRouter, Ollama, Gemini, and MiniMax
1,191 tests covering tools, agent integration, goal executor, parallel execution, worktree lifecycle, session DAG, lock correctness, spec linting, and secrets scanning

summary: Founded an AI consultancy and creative community focused on generative image AI, growing a 1,000+ member community of AI artists across Discord and DeviantArt while consulting on AI strategy and generative AI implementation.
highlights: Built a 1,000+ member community of AI artists and practitioners on Discord and DeviantArt
Developed systematic approaches to AI art direction: style guides, prompt frameworks, and model evaluation techniques
Consult for startups and creators on generative AI strategy, Flux/Stable Diffusion workflows, and LoRA fine-tuning
Write and publish on AI topics including generative models, agentic AI, and AI strategy on LinkedIn, X, and the HeartStamp blog

summary: Side project and creative space: a 10,000 sq/ft art gallery, event venue, and maker space in Batesville, AR. Built 20 original large-format musician portrait paintings (Elvis, Prince, Michael Jackson, and others) that double as DML flat-panel loudspeakers, driven by a 5-channel DSP audio system. Full maker space for wood, metal, leather, and plastic work. Hosts Oscilloscope Art/Music events where audio channels drive real-time visual animations.
highlights: Designed and built 20 DML (Distributed Mode Loudspeaker) flat-panel speakers from 4-foot foam panels with original canvas paintings of music legends — Elvis, Michael Jackson, Prince, Madonna, 2Pac, Biggie
Engineered a 5-channel audio system with DSP processing for perfectly balanced stereo coverage across the 3,000 sq/ft event space
Applied AAS Electronic Engineering background (ITT Technical Institute, 1990) to speaker design, signal chain, and crossover architecture
Hosts Oscilloscope Art/Music events: audio left/right channels drive X/Y oscilloscope inputs, creating real-time Lissajous pattern animations synchronized to live music
Full maker space for wood sculpture, metal fabrication, leather goods (hand saddle-stitched bags, backpacks, and luggage from raw leather), and plastics
Produces and sells museum-quality art prints of the musician portrait series; online presence at facebook.com/BatesvilleArt
Runs private exhibitions and events from the space

summary: Led web architecture and development for Lifeplus, an international direct sales health supplement company with operations in 40+ countries, overseeing the digital properties serving a global distributor network.
highlights: Architected and maintained web properties serving a global network of distributors across 40+ countries
Led frontend development and interactive experiences across major international markets
Transitioned the company's web stack through multiple technology generations over 13 years
Managed vendor relationships and external development teams

summary: Founded Satori Interactive in 1996 after rallying the game development team from a company layoff — immediately signing publishers Activision and Viacom. Pivoted to Flash in the early 2000s, joined ActionScript.com as a staff writer, acquired the site, and rebranded as ActionScript.com in 2003, building it into the definitive Flash developer community and consulting group.
highlights: Founded Satori Interactive in 1996, rallying the team after a layoff and immediately landing publishers Activision and Viacom — keeping everyone employed and shipping
Shipped PC game titles and mission packs (including for Redneck Rampage and Quake II) through the late 1990s
Pivoted to Flash interactive development as Flash emerged as the definitive web platform in the early 2000s
Joined ActionScript.com as a staff writer; acquired the entire site and rebranded the company as ActionScript.com in 2003
Built ActionScript.com into the largest and most recognized Flash developer community on the web
Built interactive experiences for Justin.tv (now Twitch), Bed Bath & Beyond, Frontier Airlines, and the Denver Broncos

summary: Contributed technical articles and analysis to InsideRIA.com, O'Reilly's publication covering Rich Internet Applications and the Flash/Flex/AIR ecosystem.
highlights: Wrote technical deep-dives and analysis on ActionScript, Flex, and RIA development trends
Covered the Flash ecosystem at the pivotal moment when HTML5 began to challenge Flash's dominance

summary: Lead ActionScript architect for FarmVille, the social game that became one of the most-played games in history with 80+ million monthly players at peak, fundamentally defining the social gaming category.
highlights: Designed and built the client-side ActionScript architecture for FarmVille's core gameplay systems
Architected the farm rendering system, item interaction model, and neighbor visiting mechanics
Built real-time state synchronization between the Flash client and server at social scale
FarmVille peaked at 80+ million monthly active players — the fastest-growing Facebook application at launch

summary: First Flash developer at RockYou, where I created the RockYou Slideshow — the viral social media widget that helped RockYou grow into one of the largest social media platforms of the Web 2.0 era.
highlights: First Flash developer at RockYou — built the initial Flash-based product from scratch
Created the RockYou Slideshow, a viral social media widget that drove massive user adoption
RockYou grew to become one of the largest social platforms of the Web 2.0 era with billions of widget installs
Helped establish the widget/viral loop model that defined social media growth in the Web 2.0 era

summary: Led interactive development for Lifeplus, building Flash-based training systems, product presentations, and interactive distributor tools for a global direct sales company.
highlights: Designed and built Flash-based interactive training systems for an international distributor network
Created product demonstration tools and e-commerce experiences in Flash
Managed the full interactive development lifecycle from concept through deployment

summary: Marketing director for Traditional Medicinals, one of the leading herbal tea brands in North America, overseeing brand strategy, campaigns, and the company's early web presence.
highlights: Oversaw brand strategy and marketing campaigns for a leading national herbal tea brand
Managed the company's early web presence during the first dot-com boom
Led cross-functional marketing initiatives spanning retail, wholesale, and direct channels

summary: Director of Technology at MicroStar Software, where Nuke It! reached #3 in game sales at CompUSA. The company was involved in a landmark software copyright case that set important legal precedent for the games industry.
highlights: Nuke It! reached #3 in game sales at CompUSA nationwide
Involved in a landmark software copyright case that established important legal precedent for the game industry
Managed technology development and business development at an early software startup
